This leader will define the vision, strategy, and execution across our <strong>end-to-end lead generation and marketing operating system</strong>, ensuring our customers can attract, capture, nurture, and convert leads seamlessly.This role sits at the intersection of <strong>product, growth, and platform strategy</strong>, and will oversee a broad and highly integrated portfolio of tools that power the full customer acquisition lifecycle.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>Product Scope</strong></p><p>You will lead strategy and execution across a comprehensive suite of products, including:</p><ul><li><strong>Lead Capture &amp; Conversion:</strong> Funnels &amp; Websites, Funnel AI, Forms, Surveys, Quizzes, QR Codes, Chat Widget, Countdown Timers, Funnel Widgets</li><li><strong>Traffic &amp; Acquisition:</strong> Digital Ads, SEO, AEO, GEO, Online Listings, Google Business Profile (GBP), Domains, URL Redirects</li><li><strong>Engagement &amp; Nurture:</strong> Email Marketing, Email AI, Social Planner, Blogs, Reputation Management, Notifications</li><li><strong>Growth &amp; Monetization:</strong> Affiliate Manager, Loyalty &amp; Referral Programs</li><li><strong>Brand &amp; Content Systems:</strong> Template Library, Brand Boards, Media Storage, Webinar</li><li><strong>Data, Analytics &amp; Tracking:</strong> Dashboards, Reporting, Analytics, External Tracking</li><li><strong>Emerging Experiences:</strong> Vibe and next-gen AI-driven marketing workflows</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p><p><strong>1. Vision &amp; Strategy</strong></p><ul><li>Define and own the <strong>long-term vision for organization’s Marketing OS and Lead Generation ecosystem</strong></li><li>Identify opportunities to unify fragmented tools into a <strong>cohesive, AI-powered growth platform</strong></li><li>Drive product strategy that increases <strong>lead volume, conversion rates, and customer ROI</strong></li></ul><p><strong>2. Product Leadership</strong></p><ul><li>Lead a <strong>group of product managers and cross-functional teams</strong> across multiple product lines</li><li>Ensure tight integration across all surfaces to deliver a <strong>seamless user experience</strong></li><li>Prioritize investments across a large surface area with clear <strong>ROI and customer impact</strong></li></ul><p><strong>3. Growth &amp; Monetization</strong></p><ul><li>Partner with Growth, Sales, and Customer Success to drive:</li><li>Adoption of core lead gen tools</li><li>Expansion revenue across the suite</li><li>Measurable improvements in customer acquisition performance</li><li>Develop packaging and positioning strategies for <strong>bundled and standalone offerings</strong></li></ul><p><strong>4. AI &amp; Automation Innovation</strong></p><ul><li>Embed AI across the ecosystem (Email AI, Funnels AI, Vibe, etc.) to create <strong>autonomous marketing workflows</strong></li><li>Lead the transition from tools → <strong>intelligent systems that execute for the customer</strong></li></ul><p><strong>5. Platform &amp; Ecosystem Thinking</strong></p><ul><li>Ensure all products operate as part of a unified <strong>Marketing OS</strong>, not standalone features</li><li>Drive extensibility through APIs, integrations, and ecosystem partnerships</li><li>Collaborate with other platform leaders (CRM, Payments, Conversations, etc.)</li></ul><p><strong>6. Data &amp; Performance Ownership</strong></p><ul><li>Establish and track core metrics:</li><li>Lead generation volume &amp; quality</li><li>Conversion rates across funnels</li><li>Campaign performance (ads, email, SEO)</li><li>Customer ROI and LTV impact</li><li>Build feedback loops using analytics and reporting to continuously optimize performance</li></ul><p><strong>7. Cross-Functional Leadership</strong></p><ul><li>Work closely with Engineering, Design, Marketing, Sales, and Support</li><li>Align stakeholders around roadmap priorities and platform direction</li><li>Act as a key executive voice for this product pillar internally and externally</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Qualifications</strong></p><ul><li>10+ years of experience in <strong>product management, growth, or marketing technology</strong></li><li>Proven track record leading <strong>large, complex product portfolios</strong> or platforms</li><li>Deep expertise in <strong>lead generation, digital marketing, and conversion systems</strong></li><li>Experience with tools such as:</li><li>Funnels / landing pages</li><li>Paid ads platforms</li><li>Email marketing &amp; automation</li><li>SEO / local SEO</li><li>Strong understanding of <strong>SMB and agency needs</strong> (preferred)</li><li>Experience integrating or building <strong>AI-driven product experiences</strong></li><li>Demonstrated ability to lead <strong>senior ICs and managers</strong> across multiple teams</li><li>Highly data-driven with strong business and monetization acumen</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Why This Role Matters</strong></p><p>This is one of the most strategic leadership roles at client. You’ll shape how structured data, AI/ML, and LLMs come together to build scalable, magical, and deeply relevant customer experiences at massive scale.You’ll work at the heart of a high-performing, cross-functional team—collaborating with AI researchers, data scientists, and engineers to build the foundational data products that power discovery for millions of users.This is a rare opportunity to lead in a high-agency, startup-style environment with the resources and reach of a global platform.What You’ll OwnCatalog Intelligence at Scale Lead the development of robust, intelligent, and flexible catalog systems.   "descriptionHtml": "At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.<br><br>Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.<br><br>Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.<br><br><strong>Director – Product Management, Product Knowledge<br><br></strong><strong>Build the Data-Powered, AI-Driven Future of Product Discovery<br><br></strong><strong>About The Role<br><br></strong>We’re on a mission to redefine how people discover, explore, and connect with products online—and we need a visionary product leader to take us there.<br><br>As <strong>Director of Product Management</strong>, you’ll lead the strategy and execution for our <strong>Product Knowledge and Catalog Platform</strong>—the intelligence layer that powers everything from search and recommendations to personalization and generative AI. You’ll shape how structured data, AI/ML, and LLMs come together to build <strong>scalable, magical, and deeply relevant</strong> customer experiences at massive scale.<br><br>You’ll work at the heart of a high-performing, cross-functional team—collaborating with AI researchers, data scientists, and engineers to build the foundational data products that power discovery for millions of users.<br><br>This is a rare opportunity to lead in a <strong>high-agency, startup-style environment</strong> with the resources and reach of a global platform.<br><br><strong>What You’ll Own<br><br></strong><ul><li>Catalog Intelligence at Scale Lead the development of robust, intelligent, and flexible catalog systems. Create automated, scalable ways to structure, enrich, and normalize product data across categories and geographies.</li><li>Vision for Product Knowledge Define and own the product vision for taxonomy, ontology, metadata, and knowledge graphs that power our search and AI platforms.</li><li>Build Magical Data Products Launch internal tools and APIs that feel like magic—powerful, intuitive, and built with scale and automation in mind. Make structured data work harder and smarter across all surfaces.</li><li>Power Discovery Through AI &amp; LLMs Partner with AI and data science teams to use LLMs, embeddings, and prompt engineering for entity resolution, classification, and content enrichment—fueling delightful discovery.</li><li>Design for Relevance &amp; Impact Focus relentlessly on customer experience—ensuring your products make search more relevant, recommendations smarter, and exploration more intuitive.</li><li>Drive Product Excellence Define metrics, establish feedback loops, and iterate quickly. Marry technical depth with customer empathy to shape products that solve real problems beautifully.</li><li>Collaborate to Deliver Partner across Product, Engineering, AI/ML, Trust, and Catalog Ops to launch products that are both deeply technical and profoundly user-centered.<br><br><br></li></ul><strong>What We’re Looking For<br><br></strong><ul><li>12+ years of product management experience, with 5+ years leading teams in platform, data, or AI-driven environments</li><li>Proven track record building structured data platforms, knowledge systems, or eCommerce catalog infrastructure</li><li>Strong understanding of taxonomy, metadata strategy, and product classification</li><li>Experience working with AI/ML teams, including familiarity with LLMs and semantic search (a plus)</li><li>A bias for scalable solutions—you know how to build for today and architect for tomorrow</li><li>Obsession with relevance, automation, and magic—you craft products that delight both internal and external users</li><li>Strong communicator with a track record of cross-functional leadership and influence</li><li>Comfort working in fast-paced, iterative environments—you thrive in ambiguity and move with urgency<br><br><br></li></ul><strong>Why You’ll Love This Role<br><br></strong><ul><li>Build the backbone of product discovery for one of the world’s largest eCommerce platforms</li><li>Lead a portfolio that touches AI, structured data, and real-time customer experience</li><li>Create scalable systems that drive magical user experiences at global scale</li><li>Work with world-class teams in AI/ML, data science, and engineering</li><li>Enjoy high ownership, fast execution, and a culture that values bold ideas and real impact<br><br><br></li></ul>Please see the Talent Privacy Notice for information regarding how eBay handles your personal data collected when you use the eBay Careers website or apply for a job with eBay.<br><br>eBay is an equal opportunity employer. You will manage a team of 25–30 PMs via 3 Group Product Managers and drive strategy, execution, and org design for a portfolio that underpins everything from marketing automation and conversation management to AI-driven workflows and CRM object modeling.You’ll be expected to scale a high-performance product org, influence product direction across pods, and partner closely with engineering to deliver a scalable, secure, and elegant platform for millions of users.Strategic Priorities Build an Extensible Platform: Architect reusable primitives (identity, roles, objects, notifications, reporting) that enable faster development across HighLevel. Modernize Core CRM: Scale performance and usability across contacts, companies, tasks, opportunities, and conversations — the backbone of daily SMB workflows. Unlock Scalable Automation: Advance our workflows engine and Workflow AI to power increasingly complex use cases with speed and simplicity. Drive UX Consistency Across Surfaces: Ensure UI, interaction patterns, and product language are coherent across contact, sub-account, and agency layers. Support Data-Driven Product Maturity: Enforce strong instrumentation, analytics readiness, and reporting across all core products. Operationalize Product Management Excellence: Raise the bar for product planning, prioritization, execution, and accountability across 30+ PMs and APMs.Key Responsibilities Define and drive the long term product vision, roadmap, and success metrics for platform and core product areas. Manage and coach a team ofGroup PMs and PMs,.cultivating a strong leadership bench and ensuring high performance. Set quality standards for API design, system performance, scalability, and reliability. Lead cross-functional coordination with design, engineering, QA, support, data, and legal to deliver on-time, high-quality launches. Represent the needs of agencies, sub-accounts, and end contacts in platform and CRM decisions — ensuring we build with deep user empathy. Collaborate with the Product leadership to influence org-wide product strategy, culture, and planning cadences. Lead strategic quarterly and annual planning for the org — bringing clarity to priorities across technical debt, product innovation, and user experience. Act as a thought partner to engineering leadership in shaping architecture and development investment decisions.What Success Looks Like HighLevel’s platform teams are recognised as enablers, not blockers — other pods build faster using your team's APIs, patterns, and systems. CRM primitives and workflows become more reliable, performant, and deeply adopted by our users — visible in activation and retention metrics. The organization experiences clean product ownership, predictable roadmaps, and fast decision-making under your leadership. Workflow AI and Workflow Core support a growing number of use cases without sacrificing speed, reliability, or clarity. The data model becomes flexible and extensible, enabling custom objects, relationships, and analytics without friction. You are viewed as a strategic thought partners and executional anchor for platform and core products at HighLevelIdeal Candidate Profile 12–15+ years in product management, with at least 5+ years managing managers (GPMs,Directors or equivalents). Deep fluency in CRM, automation platforms, or workflow engines — understands data models, APIs, and system-level product thinking. Prior experience building internal platforms or developer tools that support multiple product teams. Responsibilities span customer enablement during pilot and prototype phases; articulation of value propositions and requirements; partnership with internal and external development teams; commercialization and go to market planning; customer engagement and feedback loops; implementation and integration oversight; and oversight into product documentation and operational guidelines.The Role Deliver clear, consistent executive level communications by monitoring overall program health, synthesizing project and customer onboarding status, and translating insights into actionable recommendations for senior leadership and key stakeholders. Lead, mentor, and scale a global Product Operations Customer Experience team, fostering a culture of accountability, operational excellence, and coordinated execution across multiple stakeholders and parallel initiatives. Define and champion the strategic vision for the Product Operations Customer Experience team, ensuring alignment with enterprise objectives while continuously evolving delivery models, processes, and governance to support long term scalability and success. Partner with internal stakeholders to support product management and development efforts across all new and enhanced Network Product solutions, including piloting and testing activities, and co developing customer communications, guides, and operational documentation. Engage with internal product teams, business owners, regional market teams, and global customers to identify, assess, and address network challenges and opportunities. Analyze internal data to size opportunities and issues, understand customer behaviors, and develop informed perspectives on system, platform, and technical constraints underlying business needs. Collaborate with a diverse set of internal partners, regional stakeholders, and external customers to define, deliver, and continuously improve the end to end customer onboarding experience. Lead customer feedback engagements to identify pain points, inform roadmap prioritization, and support proactive customer management during incidents or service disruptions. Demonstrate intellectual curiosity, high energy, and a self starter mindset, with the ability to operate with urgency in a fast paced, evolving environment. Exhibit strong prioritization discipline and comfort operating in ambiguity, while empowering teams to own and deliver a high quality customer onboarding experience. Effectively manage multiple priorities and complex demands, supported by a strong understanding of transaction processing and the credit card payments industry.About You Brings deep expertise in the payments industry, the Mastercard ecosystem, or the broader financial services landscape, with a strong understanding of how technology delivery drives business outcomes. Operates with a high degree of independence and accountability, demonstrating sound judgment and end to end ownership of program delivery. Exhibits a self starter mindset with a strong ability to learn quickly, work independently, thrive in fast paced environments, and adapt to shifting priorities while managing multiple responsibilities.   "description": "About LearneoLearneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal. Role OverviewWe are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.ResponsibilitiesStrategy & LeadershipOrganic Growth Strategy: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales. Team Leadership: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO & Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.Roadmap Ownership: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.Cross-Functional Partnership: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.Product Roadmap Influence: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.Search Engine Optimization (SEO)SEO Program Ownership: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.Technical SEO: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.Content & Keyword Strategy: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.Link Building & Authority: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.Algorithm Resilience: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)GEO Strategy: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).Content for LLMs: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.Measurement & Experimentation: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.Competitive Intelligence: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.App Store Optimisation (ASO)ASO Program Ownership: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.Listing Optimization: Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.Ratings & Reviews: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.A/B Testing: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion.   "descriptionHtml": "<p><strong>About Learneo<br></strong>Learneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal.</p> <p><strong>Role Overview<br></strong>We are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO &amp; Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.</p><p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Strategy &amp; Leadership</strong><ul><li><strong>Organic Growth Strategy</strong>: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales.&nbsp;</li><li><strong>Team Leadership</strong>: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO &amp; Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.</li><li><strong>Roadmap Ownership</strong>: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.</li><li><strong>Cross-Functional Partnership</strong>: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.</li><li><strong>Product Roadmap Influence</strong>: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Search Engine Optimization (SEO)<br></strong><ul><li><strong>SEO Program Ownership</strong>: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.</li><li><strong>Technical SEO</strong>: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.</li><li><strong>Content &amp; Keyword Strategy</strong>: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.</li><li><strong>Link Building &amp; Authority</strong>: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.</li><li><strong>Algorithm Resilience</strong>: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)</strong><ul><li><strong>GEO Strategy</strong>: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).</li><li><strong>Content for LLMs</strong>: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.</li><li><strong>Measurement &amp; Experimentation</strong>: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.</li><li><strong>Competitive Intelligence</strong>: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.</li></ul></li><li><strong>App Store Optimisation (ASO)</strong><ul><li><strong>ASO Program Ownership</strong>: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.</li><li><strong>Listing Optimization:</strong> Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.</li><li><strong>Ratings &amp; Reviews</strong>: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.</li><li><strong>A/B Testing</strong>: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.</li><li><strong>Market-Specific ASO</strong>: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Extension Store Optimisation</strong><ul><li><strong>Extension Visibility</strong>: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.</li><li><strong>Listing &amp; CRO</strong>: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.</li><li><strong>Ratings &amp; Review Strategy</strong>: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.</li><li><strong>Cross-Channel Synergy</strong>: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Analytics, Reporting &amp; Insights</strong><ul><li><strong>Performance Measurement</strong>: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.</li><li><strong>Executive Reporting</strong>: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. relationships with customer C‑suite and internal stakeholders across functions</li><li>Collaborate closely with Services Sales Pre-Sales, Services Delivery, Product Management, Customer Success, and Ethos is redefining how life insurance is bought, sold, and underwritten.About The RoleWe’re looking for a Director of Product Design to lead and elevate design for Ethos’s core business — the end-to-end journey that takes customers from landing page to policy activation and beyond.   "description": "About LearneoLearneo is a platform of builder-driven businesses, including Course Hero, CliffsNotes, LitCharts, Quillbot, Symbolab, and Scribbr, all united around a shared mission of supercharging productivity and learning for everyone. We attract and scale high growth businesses built and run by visionary entrepreneurs. Each team innovates independently but has a unique opportunity to collaborate, experiment, and grow together, and they are supported by centralized corporate operations functions, including HR, Finance and Legal. Role OverviewWe are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.ResponsibilitiesStrategy & LeadershipOrganic Growth Strategy: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales. Team Leadership: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO & Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.Roadmap Ownership: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.Cross-Functional Partnership: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.Product Roadmap Influence: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.Search Engine Optimization (SEO)SEO Program Ownership: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.Technical SEO: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.Content & Keyword Strategy: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.Link Building & Authority: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.Algorithm Resilience: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)GEO Strategy: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).Content for LLMs: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.Measurement & Experimentation: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.Competitive Intelligence: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.App Store Optimisation (ASO)ASO Program Ownership: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.Listing Optimization: Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.Ratings & Reviews: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.A/B Testing: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.Market-Specific ASO: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.Extension Store OptimisationExtension Visibility: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.Listing & CRO: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.Ratings & Review Strategy: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.Cross-Channel Synergy: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.Analytics, Reporting & InsightsPerformance Measurement: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.Executive Reporting: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. Translate complex organic data into clear narratives with actionable recommendations.Tool Stack: Evaluate and manage the organic growth tool stack (e.g., Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Sensor Tower, Screaming Frog, etc).Qualifications8–10 years of progressive experience in SEO and/or organic growth, with at least 3 years in a people-management role.Global experience: demonstrated ability to drive organic growth across multiple markets, regions, and languages—not limited to English-speaking audiences.Demonstrated success managing organic growth for a high-traffic website (+20M monthly active users) and a consumer app with significant install volume.Proven track record of securing and defending #1 positions for competitive keywordsProven ability to build organic visibility from zero for new product launches in highly competitive markets — taking a product from no search presence to top-ranking positions against established incumbents.Deep expertise across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO, with a strong grasp of how search engines crawl, index, and rank content.Hands-on experience with ASO for iOS and/or Android apps, including keyword optimization, listing experiments, and review management.Strong understanding of the generative AI search landscape and emerging GEO best practices.Proficiency with industry tools: Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and at least one ASO platform (preferably Sensor Tower).Experience working with CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress) and comfort with basic HTML/CSS.Excellent communicator who can present data-driven strategies to senior leadership, including C-level stakeholders.Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related   "description": "About UsQuillBot was founded in 2017 with a mission to help students and professionals strengthen their writing. Today, we help over 56 million people around the world create great things. Whether you're writing, designing, coding, or collaborating, QuillBot is a place where anyone can create at the speed of thought. Our AI-powered tools help you think clearly, communicate effectively, and create beautifully—across every platform, in any format, at any skill level. If you're passionate about using technology to make the path from inspiration to execution more accessible, intentional, and relevant, come join us. Role OverviewWe are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.ResponsibilitiesStrategy & LeadershipOrganic Growth Strategy: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales. Team Leadership: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO & Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.Roadmap Ownership: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.Cross-Functional Partnership: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.Product Roadmap Influence: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.Search Engine Optimization (SEO)SEO Program Ownership: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.Technical SEO: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.Content & Keyword Strategy: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.Link Building & Authority: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.Algorithm Resilience: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)GEO Strategy: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).Content for LLMs: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.Measurement & Experimentation: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.Competitive Intelligence: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.App Store Optimisation (ASO)ASO Program Ownership: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.Listing Optimization: Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.Ratings & Reviews: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.A/B Testing: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.Market-Specific ASO: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.Extension Store OptimisationExtension Visibility: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.Listing & CRO: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.Ratings & Review Strategy: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.Cross-Channel Synergy: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.Analytics, Reporting & InsightsPerformance Measurement: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.Executive Reporting: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. Translate complex organic data into clear narratives with actionable recommendations.Tool Stack: Evaluate and manage the organic growth tool stack (e.g., Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Sensor Tower, Screaming Frog, etc).Qualifications8–10 years of progressive experience in SEO and/or organic growth, with at least 3 years in a people-management role.Global experience: demonstrated ability to drive organic growth across multiple markets, regions, and languages—not limited to English-speaking audiences.Demonstrated success managing organic growth for a high-traffic website (+20M monthly active users) and a consumer app with significant install volume.Proven track record of securing and defending #1 positions for competitive keywordsProven ability to build organic visibility from zero for new product launches in highly competitive markets — taking a product from no search presence to top-ranking positions against established incumbents.Deep expertise across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO, with a strong grasp of how search engines crawl, index, and rank content.Hands-on experience with ASO for iOS and/or Android apps, including keyword optimization, listing experiments, and review management.Strong understanding of the generative AI search landscape and emerging GEO best practices.Proficiency with industry tools: Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Screaming Frog, and at least one ASO platform (preferably Sensor Tower).Experience working with CMS platforms (e.g., WordPress) and comfort with basic HTML/CSS.Excellent communicator who can present data-driven strategies to senior leadership, including C-level stakeholders.Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.Bonus PointsExperience at a SaaS, EdTech, or consumer-tech company with a freemium or subscription business model.Hands-on experience with multilingual and multi-regional SEO and ASO strategies.Familiarity with structured data, schema markup, and Knowledge Graph optimization.Experience with programmatic SEO or large-scale page generation strategies.Experience optimizing browser extension listings in the Chrome Web Store or equivalent platforms.Background in conversion rate optimization (CRO) as it relates to organic landing pages and store listings.Comfortable working across time zones in a fully remote, globally distributed team.Why Join QuillBot?Ownership & Impact: Take full ownership of organic growth across every discovery surface and directly influence QuillBot’s trajectory.Scope & Challenge: Lead a function that spans SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Stores—a rare opportunity to shape organic strategy holistically.Collaborative Culture: Work with a passionate, innovative team across Content, Marketing, Product, and Engineering.Remote Flexibility: Enjoy the flexibility of working remotely with flexible hours to balance professional and personal   "descriptionHtml": "<p><strong><strong>About Us<br></strong></strong>QuillBot was founded in 2017 with a mission to help students and professionals strengthen their writing. Today, we help over 56 million people around the world create great things. Whether you're writing, designing, coding, or collaborating, QuillBot is a place where anyone can create at the speed of thought. Our AI-powered tools help you think clearly, communicate effectively, and create beautifully—across every platform, in any format, at any skill level. If you're passionate about using technology to make the path from inspiration to execution more accessible, intentional, and relevant, come join us.</p> <p><strong>Role Overview<br></strong>We are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO &amp; Organic Growth team. You’ll partner closely with Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, and International teams to embed organic growth into QuillBot’s product and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence organic traffic, sales, install volume, revenue, and brand visibility on a global scale.</p><p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Strategy &amp; Leadership</strong><ul><li><strong>Organic Growth Strategy</strong>: Define and own the overarching organic growth strategy spanning SEO, ASO, GEO, and Extension Store optimization. Set quarterly and annual OKRs tied to impressions, clicks, MAU, installs, and sales.&nbsp;</li><li><strong>Team Leadership</strong>: Manage, mentor, and develop the SEO &amp; Organic Growth team. Foster a culture of experimentation, accountability, and continuous learning. Identify and hire for key capability gaps as needed.</li><li><strong>Roadmap Ownership</strong>: Build and maintain a prioritized organic growth roadmap. Balance quick wins with longer-term strategic bets. Present plans and progress to senior leadership, including the CEO.</li><li><strong>Cross-Functional Partnership</strong>: Serve as the organic growth voice in cross-functional planning with AI, Product, Engineering, Content, Brand, Marketing Operations, and International Expansion teams. Ensure organic considerations are embedded in product launches, site architecture decisions, and content strategy.</li><li><strong>Product Roadmap Influence</strong>: Act as a key influencer of the Product roadmap by surfacing organic growth opportunities, user intent data, and search landscape insights that shape feature prioritization, new product bets, and go-to-market timing.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Search Engine Optimization (SEO)<br></strong><ul><li><strong>SEO Program Ownership</strong>: Own end-to-end SEO performance across QuillBot’s web properties. Drive strategy across on-page, off-page, and technical SEO to secure and defend top positions for high-value keywords.</li><li><strong>Technical SEO</strong>: Lead technical audits and partner with Engineering to resolve crawlability, indexation, Core Web Vitals, site speed, structured data, and mobile-responsiveness issues at scale.</li><li><strong>Content &amp; Keyword Strategy</strong>: Oversee keyword research, content gap analysis, and on-page optimization. Collaborate with the Content team to ensure every page is strategically targeted and continuously improved.</li><li><strong>Link Building &amp; Authority</strong>: Direct link-building and digital PR strategies to grow domain authority. Evaluate backlink quality and build partnerships with authoritative sites.</li><li><strong>Algorithm Resilience</strong>: Monitor Google algorithm updates, diagnose ranking fluctuations, and quickly adapt strategies to protect and grow organic traffic.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO)</strong><ul><li><strong>GEO Strategy</strong>: Develop and execute a strategy to maximize QuillBot’s visibility in AI-generated answers and citations across generative search experiences (e.g., Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, etc).</li><li><strong>Content for LLMs</strong>: Work with Content and Product to structure information, schema markup, and authoritative content so that generative engines accurately surface and recommend QuillBot.</li><li><strong>Measurement &amp; Experimentation</strong>: Define GEO-specific metrics, build tracking frameworks, and run experiments to understand how generative surfaces drive traffic and conversions.</li><li><strong>Competitive Intelligence</strong>: Monitor how competitors are cited in generative answers and identify opportunities to increase QuillBot’s share of voice.</li></ul></li><li><strong>App Store Optimisation (ASO)</strong><ul><li><strong>ASO Program Ownership</strong>: Own QuillBot’s organic discoverability and conversion rates in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.</li><li><strong>Listing Optimization:</strong> Optimize app titles, subtitles, descriptions, keywords, screenshots, and preview videos to improve search rankings and download conversion rates.</li><li><strong>Ratings &amp; Reviews</strong>: Develop strategies to increase the volume and quality of positive ratings and reviews, and work with Product to address recurring user feedback themes.</li><li><strong>A/B Testing</strong>: Design and run store listing experiments to continuously improve conversion. Leverage tools like Google Play Experiments and third-party ASO platforms.</li><li><strong>Market-Specific ASO</strong>: Partner with the International Expansion team to localize and optimize listings for priority markets.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Extension Store Optimisation</strong><ul><li><strong>Extension Visibility</strong>: Own QuillBot’s presence and discoverability in the Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, Microsoft Edge Add-ons, and Firefox Add-ons.</li><li><strong>Listing &amp; CRO</strong>: Optimize extension titles, descriptions, screenshots, and feature graphics. Improve install conversion rates through iterative testing.</li><li><strong>Ratings &amp; Review Strategy</strong>: Drive rating volume and quality for extensions. Coordinate with Product and Support to address negative feedback and reduce churn.</li><li><strong>Cross-Channel Synergy</strong>: Align extension store strategy with SEO and ASO efforts to create a unified organic discovery experience across web, app, and extension surfaces.</li></ul></li><li><strong>Analytics, Reporting &amp; Insights</strong><ul><li><strong>Performance Measurement</strong>: Build and maintain dashboards tracking organic performance across all channels (SEO, ASO, GEO, Extension Stores). Define KPIs and ensure the team is data-driven in decision-making.</li><li><strong>Executive Reporting</strong>: Deliver regular performance updates and strategic insights to senior leadership. Translate complex organic data into clear narratives with actionable recommendations.</li><li><strong>Tool Stack</strong>: Evaluate and manage the organic growth tool stack (e.g., Google Analytics, Google Search Console, SEMrush, Ahrefs, Sensor Tower, Screaming Frog, etc).</li></ul></li></ul><p><strong>Qualifications</strong></p><ul><li>8–10 years of progressive experience in SEO and/or organic Role OverviewWe are looking for a strategic, high-ownership Director of Organic Growth to lead and scale QuillBot’s organic acquisition engine across all major discovery surfaces. This role goes well beyond traditional SEO—you will own the full spectrum of organic growth, including Search Engine Optimization (SEO), App Store Optimization (ASO), Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), and Extension Store visibility (Chrome Web Store, Safari Extensions Gallery, and others). As a Director, you will set the vision, build and manage the roadmap, and lead the existing SEO & Organic Growth team. You'll set the technical roadmap and engineering standards, remove obstacles to team performance, and partner across Product, Design, and Operations. Anchored in our core values of Make it Happen, Serve, and One Team, you'll drive data-driven decisions that boost speed, quality, reliability, and cost efficiency—while fostering innovation, inclusion, and continuous learning.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p><ul><li>Set and execute the engineering strategy for the Advisor Platform; build succession plans; cultivate an inclusive, high-performance culture.</li><li>Own delivery excellence across the SDLC; standardize Agile/DevOps practices (CI/CD, testing, observability, incident/postmortems) to improve reliability, security, and maintainability.</li><li>Make accountable, data-driven decisions on resourcing, budget, portfolio priorities, and sequencing; optimize total cost of ownership for platform components.</li><li>Establish and enforce engineering standards and controls (secure coding, data protection, change management, access governance) and carry governance accountability for quality, security, and maintainability.</li><li>Influence peer leaders and senior stakeholders across Product, Design, Data, Operations, Security, and Compliance; provide clear narratives and metrics to guide trade-offs and decisions.</li><li>Maintain deep domain awareness of wealth management regulations and client sensitivities (e.g., data privacy, suitability, fiduciary obligations); partner with Compliance to keep standards current.</li><li>Proactively identify and mitigate risk; lead incident response and root cause remediation; ensure all solutions adhere to internal standards and applicable regulatory guidelines.</li><li>Drive architecture and modernization initiatives (cloud/platform evolution, build vs. buy, legacy remediation) that increase scalability, resilience, and speed of delivery.</li></ul><p><br></p><p>What You Bring</p><ul><li><strong>Engineering leadership at scale</strong>: Proven track record leading manager-of-managers organizations and developing high-performing teams (hiring, coaching, succession, inclusion).</li><li><strong>Modern SDLC &amp; delivery excellence</strong>: Deep familiarity with Agile, DevOps, CI/CD, testing/automation, observability, incident response/postmortems, and reliability practices.</li><li><strong>Architecture &amp; cloud</strong>: Proven experience designing and delivering large-scale, cloud-native platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP) with strong governance (security, reliability, observability) and cost-of-ownership optimization.</li><li><strong>Security &amp; risk</strong>: Strong knowledge of secure coding, compliance, and risk management within technology organizations; able to embed controls throughout the SDLC.</li><li><strong>Stakeholder influence</strong>: Demonstrated ability to collaborate across matrixed environments and influence senior stakeholders using clear narratives and data.</li><li><strong>Operational judgment</strong>: Strong analytical/problem-solving skills with hands-on experience in incident management and issue resolution; comfort making data-driven trade-offs across scope, schedule, and quality.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Requirements</strong></p><ul><li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).</li><li>17+ years of significant, progressively responsible experience in software engineering including 5+ years leading managers/technologists to anticipate and solve complex technical and operational challenges. You will manage a small team and serve as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, influencing decisions that impact our global workforce.  Why join Roku in Bangalore? Our newly established Bengaluru office represents an exciting chapter in Roku’s global expansion. hubs. You’ll work in a collaborative environment that values innovation, transparency, and data-driven decision-making, while having a direct line to global leaders If you thrive on creating We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We believe a fewer number of very talented folks can do more for less cost than a larger number of less talented teams. We're independent thinkers with big ideas who act boldly, move fast and accomplish extraordinary things through collaboration and trust. The Director is accountable for embedding security controls into modern cloud‑native architectures while supporting regulatory compliance, customer assurance, and business growth.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p><p><br></p><p><strong>1. Product Security Leadership</strong></p><ul><li>Define and execute the <strong>Product Security strategy</strong> across design, development, testing, release, and operations.</li><li>Embed security into <strong>SDLC and CI/CD pipelines</strong>, ensuring consistent adoption across engineering teams.</li><li>Lead <strong>threat modeling, secure architecture reviews, and design risk assessments</strong> for new and existing product capabilities.</li><li>Govern <strong>application security testing programs</strong>, including: </li><li>SAST, DAST, and SCA</li><li>Infrastructure‑as‑Code (IaC) and API security testing</li><li>Own <strong>product vulnerability management</strong>, including prioritization, remediation SLAs, and customer‑facing disclosures.</li><li>Partner with Product and Engineering leadership to ensure security supports <strong>speed, scale, and innovation</strong>.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>2. Cloud &amp; Platform Security</strong></p><ul><li>Own security architecture and posture for <strong>cloud‑native SaaS platforms hosted on Microsoft Azure</strong>.</li><li>Define and enforce <strong>cloud security guardrails</strong> across: </li><li>Identity &amp; access management (IAM, MFA, PAM)</li><li>Network security, WAFs, and segmentation</li><li>Encryption, key management, logging, and monitoring</li><li>Lead <strong>Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM)</strong> and continuous configuration assurance.</li><li>Oversee security for <strong>containers, Kubernetes, APIs, and microservices</strong>.</li><li>Ensure effective integration and use of core security platforms, including: </li><li><strong>Azure Security Center / Defender</strong></li><li><strong>FortiGate Firewall &amp; FortiWeb WAF</strong></li><li><strong>CyberArk (PAM)</strong></li><li><strong>Lacework (CSPM)</strong></li><li><strong>Sumo Logic (SIEM)</strong></li><li><strong>Datadog (APM &amp; monitoring)</strong></li><li><strong>CrowdStrike (endpoint &amp; workload protection)</strong></li><li><strong>Tenable (vulnerability management)</strong></li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>3. AI Security &amp; Secure AI Integration (New – Core Accountability)</strong></p><ul><li>Define and own the <strong>AI Security strategy</strong> for product features leveraging AI/ML and GenAI capabilities.</li><li>Ensure AI features are designed and deployed in line with <strong>Responsible AI, privacy, and security‑by‑design principles</strong>.</li><li>Partner with Product and Engineering teams to: </li><li>Perform <strong>AI threat modeling</strong> (model abuse, prompt injection, data leakage, training data risks).</li><li>Establish <strong>guardrails for AI usage</strong>, including data classification, access controls, and output validation.</li><li>Govern security controls for: </li><li>Training data protection and lineage</li><li>Model integrity and lifecycle management</li><li>Secure integration of third‑party AI services and APIs</li><li>Align AI security practices with: </li><li>Internal <strong>AI Security and Responsible Use policies</strong></li><li>Emerging regulations and frameworks (e.g., EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, data protection laws)</li><li>Act as a key member of the <strong>AI Governance Committee</strong>, providing security and risk leadership.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>4. Governance, Risk &amp; Compliance (Product &amp; Cloud Scope)</strong></p><ul><li>Ensure product and cloud controls meet <strong>ISO 27001/27017, SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, DPDPA</strong>, and customer contractual requirements.</li><li>Support <strong>customer security reviews, RFPs, audits, and due‑diligence</strong> activities.</li><li>Translate regulatory and contractual obligations into <strong>practical, auditable technical controls</strong>.</li><li>Maintain strong documentation, evidence, and metrics for audits and certifications.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>5. Incident Response &amp; Security Operations</strong></p><ul><li>Act as a senior escalation point for <strong>product and cloud security incidents</strong>.</li><li>Ensure effective detection, response, root‑cause analysis, and post‑incident improvements.</li><li>Partner with SOC and engineering teams to improve <strong>mean‑time‑to‑detect and remediate</strong>.</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>6.   "descriptionHtml": "<p><strong>About the Company</strong></p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p>Founded in 2015, Rapido is India’s leading multi-modal mobility platform. What began with bike taxis has rapidly evolved to include auto-rickshaws, cab-hailing, and peer-to-peer deliveries. Today, Rapido commands a dominant 70% market share in bike taxis, 40% in auto rides, and holds the second-largest position in the cab segment with a 22% share—establishing us as the overall market leader in ride-hailing. With over 4 million rides completed daily and more than 2 billion rides served to date, Rapido operates in 200+ cities and is aggressively expanding toward a presence in 500 cities. In 2024, we proudly achieved unicorn status with a valuation of $1.1 billion. Driven by technology and innovation, we’re expanding into new categories while remaining committed to improving last-mile connectivity and empowering livelihoods at scale. We are not an option; we are a choice!</p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p><strong>About the Role</strong></p><p><br></p><p><br></p><p>As a Regional Manager you are responsible for growing and maintaining reliable service delivery of your cities.   "description": "Our PurposeMastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.Title And SummaryVice President - Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance)Who is Mastercard?Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships, and innovation, we help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture of inclusion and respect. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.OverviewAs Vice President, Enterprise Value Quantification (MVQ Program Management & Governance), you will lead Mastercard’s enterprise-wide value quantification program – scaling and unifying our current Services Value Quantification (SVQ) into a holistic Mastercard Value Quantification (MVQ) capability across Core, Commercial & New Payment Flows (CNPF), and Services. Reporting to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, you will own the program’s strategy, governance, and execution. Your mission is to embed a value-first culture and ensure that quantifying customer value is at the heart of how Mastercard designs, sells, and delivers its solutions. You will establish a scalable, persona-based MVQ framework and platform to provide tailored value narratives and ROI insights. This role serves as a critical enterprise connector – collaborating across Product, Sales, Pricing, Customer Success, Finance, Technology, and regional teams – to drive broad adoption and tangible impact of MVQ globally. (This position reports to the Senior Vice President, Value Enablement & Optimization, within Mastercard’s Services organization.)Key Responsibilities Enterprise MVQ Vision & Leadership: Define and champion the enterprise MVQ vision and roadmap. Lead the evolution of SVQ into one unified MVQ program that quantifies and communicates the value of Mastercard’s solutions across all lines of business. Program Management & Governance: Establish and chair an MVQ governance framework that aligns cross-functional priorities, standardizes methodologies, and eliminates duplicative value quantification efforts. Implement a disciplined operating model with clear priorities and accountability. Holistic Value Story & Scalable Platform: Develop Mastercard’s cohesive value story with persona-specific value propositions and ROI narratives for key audiences (e.g., account teams, product teams, pricing, executives). Oversee MVQ platform enhancements to deliver these insights at scale, enabling users to generate tailored, customer-ready value analyses. Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with cross-functional teams – including Product, Sales (Account Management Customer Solutions Center, Services Business Leads), Pricing, Customer Success, and others – to embed MVQ into product, pricing, sales, and customer engagement processes, driving a unified, value-focused approach across the customer lifecycle. Adoption & Enablement: Drive enterprise adoption of MVQ and practices. Develop training, communications, and support programs to integrate MVQ into the daily workflows of client-facing teams. Use feedback and metrics to continually improve the platform and value delivery. Platform & Data Stewardship: Guide the ongoing development of the MVQ platform, working with Technology and Analytics partners to integrate new data sources, product value models, and analytical capabilities. Ensure the platform is robust, intuitive, and compliant with Mastercard’s standards. People Leadership: Build and lead a high-performing MVQ team. Provide mentorship, coaching, and clear direction to develop talent and expertise. Foster a collaborative, innovative, and results-driven culture that embraces a value-first mindset and grows the next generation of value experts.Key Skills & Qualifications Extensive experience: 15+ years in strategy, program management, consulting, or related roles leading large-scale, cross-functional initiatives in complex organizations (payments, financial services, or B2B technology sector preferred). Strategic & analytical acumen: Proven strength in strategic thinking, financial analysis, and data-driven problem solving. Adept at developing ROI models and translating analytics into actionable business insights. Value & commercial expertise: Familiarity with value-based selling, pricing strategy, and customer value management. Ability to articulate and quantify the business value of technology or service solutions to customers and internal stakeholders. Leadership & influence: Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence senior executives and diverse stakeholders. Skilled at driving alignment in a matrixed environment through vision and collaboration. Change management: Demonstrated success in driving adoption of new tools, processes, or cultural initiatives at scale. Passion for evangelizing innovation, with a track record of leading organizational change and embedding new practices. People development: Strong people management capabilities. Experience building and mentoring high-performing teams, developing talent, and fostering an inclusive, high-energy culture.Leadership Attributes Visionary & enterprise-minded: Strategic thinker who sees the big picture and can articulate a compelling, forward-looking vision for value quantification as a competitive differentiator. Collaborative influencer: Excels at leading through influence and building consensus. Cultivates trust and teamwork across functions, geographies, and levels to achieve shared goals. Results-focused & accountable: Driven to deliver tangible results. Sets high standards, measures progress and takes ownership to ensure execution and business impact. Innovative & adaptable: Embraces change with a continuous improvement mindset. Stays ahead of industry trends (e.g., data analytics, AI) to evolve capabilities and encourage creative solutions. Authentic & inclusive leader: Exemplifies Mastercard’s values and Decency Quotient (DQ). Creates a culture of integrity, inclusion, and empowerment. Inspires and develops others through mentorship and leading by example.Success Measures Widespread Adoption: MVQ is widely utilized across Mastercard’s businesses, with strong engagement from product, sales, account management, and customer success teams globally. Measurable Impact: MVQ drives tangible commercial outcomes – influencing key deal wins, supporting value-based pricing and renewals, and helping demonstrate ROI to customers – resulting in improved client satisfaction, retention, and revenue growth. Executive Alignment: MVQ is championed by senior leadership as a critical enterprise capability. Its insights are regularly incorporated into strategic planning, business reviews, and go-to-market decisions, reinforcing Mastercard’s value-driven culture and competitive differentiation.Corporate Security Responsibility All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;Report any suspected information security violation or breach, andComplete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.",
